facts: An environment changes when the season changes; in winter, some places get cold and snow covers the ground, so animals like birds migrate to warmer places.
Living things like plants and animals can adapt to changes in their habitat; for example, a tree loses its leaves in autumn to survive the cold winter.
Non-living things in an environment, like rocks and water, do not move or grow, but they can change when the weather changes, such as water turning to ice in winter.
misconceptions: A child may think that all animals stay in the same place all year long, not realizing that some animals migrate to find food or warmer weather when the environment changes.
